description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a dark, metallic finish and a robust, symmetrical design. The axe features two large, crescent-shaped blades on either side of a central hub, each blade adorned with subtle decorative ridges and a slightly beveled edge. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and ornately detailed with multiple raised bands and a flared pommel at the base, providing both grip and visual interest. The central shaft appears to be made of the same dark metal as the blades, giving the entire object a monolithic, forged appearance. The overall aesthetic is imposing and functional, with a medieval or fantasy-inspired style suitable for display or ceremonial use. No visible markings, engravings, or brand labels are present on the surface.
description: A double-headed, dark metallic battle axe with ornate blades and a detailed, straight handle. The design is symmetrical, robust, and visually striking.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, crescent-shaped blades and the full ornate handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the straight, cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying both blades and the handle's decorative features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the handle's symmetry.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical metal handle with raised bands and a flared pommel, designed for grip and durability.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped blades with beveled edges and subtle decorative ridges, symmetrical and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Integrated forged mounting, with the blade and handle appearing as a single piece.
